title: Support `kind: NetworkPolicy`
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind feature

**Description**

See https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/network-policies/

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
N/A

**Describe the results you received:**
Not supported.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Being supported.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I prefer to use `podman play kube` with `yaml`-files.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```

Version:      4.0.1

Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Sun Mar 13 12:44:23 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Requested by https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12965#issuecomment-1069231166
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove Stale
@Luap99 PTAL
This is a interesting use case, however I think there are more important networking features that we have to implement first.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove Stale
@Luap99 something an intern could work on?

This will be a huge amount of effort to get this working. There is no design concept and I have no idea how this should look like.
(CNI, netavark, rootless/root, slirp4netns, etc...) There are just too many variables involved.
I think there are a lot of networking things with a higher priority.
Ok
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove Stale
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove Stale
@mheon PTAL
Looks like a substantial amount of work to get this implemented, probably will need a card.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
/remove Stale

Due to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/16029 having been merged just a few hours ago, I think the need for this feature has grown bigger.
In effect, this now seems to be the only way to prevent 2 pods from communicating with one another (as they can now do so freely over the default auto-attached `podman-default-kube-network` network).

> Due to #16029 having been merged just a few hours ago, I think the need for this feature has grown bigger. In effect, this now seems to be the only way to prevent 2 pods from communicating with one another (as they can now do so freely over the default auto-attached `podman-default-kube-network` network).

This was already the case when run as root. To restore the previous behaviour as rootless you can still use --network slirp4netns.
